creator_thesis: @bjornd.al is a Minneapolis-based multi-hyphenate (musician, graphic designer, writer, video essayist) who uses absurdist humor as the connective tissue across wildly disparate creative output. His primary vehicle is "Esoteric Meme Breakdown," a video series that uses internet memes as entry points into obscure history, conspiracy lore, and art criticism. Simultaneously, he documents a dense local DIY scene through band promotion, self-published chapbooks, invented holidays, and hand-made merchandise — all delivered with a deadpan, self-aware voice.

key_concepts.name: Esoteric Meme Breakdown
key_concepts.definition: A serialized video series that uses internet memes as entry points into obscure history, lost knowledge, and art criticism — memes as Trojan horses for intellectual content.

key_concepts.name: Lost-knowledge deep-dives
key_concepts.definition: Recurring investigative interest in forgotten or discredited innovators whose techniques or contributions were suppressed, erased, or died with them — treated as genuine historical tragedy.

key_concepts.name: DIY music documentation
key_concepts.definition: Sustained, community-minded coverage of Minneapolis's indie and experimental music scene — bands, venues, residencies, and concert graphics treated as worth archiving and celebrating.

key_concepts.name: Absurdist community mythology
key_concepts.definition: Invented holidays, mascots, rituals, and in-jokes that translate online deadpan humor into real-world IRL gatherings and communal identity.

key_concepts.name: Self-publishing and zine culture
key_concepts.definition: Chapbooks, hand-bound editions, free digital reads, and small-batch signed copies as the primary publishing model — distribution through shows and mail, priced at five to eight dollars.

key_concepts.name: Graphic design as artistic practice
key_concepts.definition: Posters, album art, book layouts, merchandise, and mascots treated as standalone creative output rather than purely functional deliverables.

key_concepts.name: Serialized brand infrastructure
key_concepts.definition: Episode numbering, recurring mascots, a dedicated shop, and merchandise function as the organizational scaffolding that turns a web series into a persistent creative world.

key_concepts.name: Deadpan absurdism as tonal default
key_concepts.definition: Even functional announcements — show dates, album drops, holiday logistics — are filtered through ironic, self-deprecating comedy that never fully drops the bit.

positions: Memes are legitimate intellectual vehicles — the absurd format earns its depth rather than undercutting it.

Working around limitations is a path to mastery: Irving Berlin's inability to play outside F-sharp led him to commission transposing pianos and become one of the most prolific songwriters in American history.
Lost or suppressed knowledge (Segato's petrification method, destroyed upon his death) is worth investigating and mourning — these gaps in the historical record are genuinely tragic.
Community infrastructure — invented holidays, hand-made licenses, mascots — can be built through humor without sacrificing sincerity; the bit and the real thing coexist.
Self-deprecating irony ('I am going to become an influencer now') is fully compatible with serious, sustained craft across multiple disciplines.
